title: can`t start chronyd.service in container
body:
### Issue Description

I use roootles mode, and try to run container in rootless mode:
[podman@worker ~]$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.4.1
API Version:  4.4.1
Go Version:   go1.19.4

OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

podman-4.4.1-12.module+el8.8.0+1265+fa25dd7a.x86_64

Hello dear colleagues!
I have some problems, with chronyd.service in my containers.
I run containers with all capabilities (and try run with only SYS-TIME capabilities), and chronyd.service service does\`t start.
Service failed, and report "chronyd[20]: adjtimex(0x8001) failed : Operation not permitted",
i search with problem on [https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1772053](url), but it didn't help me.
I use to try run different command:

`podman run -d --cap-add=ALL --name rocky mymirror.localhost/rocky8`
`podman run -d --cap-add=CAP_SYS_TIME --name rocky mymirror.localhost/rocky8` 
`podman run -d --cap-add=ALL --privileged --name rocky mymirror.localhost/rocky8`

In all cases, service chronyd.service doesn`t work:

`[podman@worker ~]$ podman exec -it rocky bash`
`[root@6350b2e720e9 /]# systemctl status chronyd.service`
● chronyd.service - NTP client/server
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/system/chronyd.service; enabled; vendor preset: enabled)
   Active: failed (Result: exit-code) since Mon 2023-08-28 13:57:25 MSK; 13s ago
     Docs: man:chronyd(8)
           man:chrony.conf(5)
  Process: 29 ExecStopPost=/usr/libexec/chrony-helper remove-daemon-state (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
  Process: 20 ExecStart=/usr/sbin/chronyd $OPTIONS (code=exited, status=1/FAILURE)

Aug 28 13:57:25 6350b2e720e9 systemd[1]: Starting NTP client/server...
Aug 28 13:57:25 6350b2e720e9 chronyd[20]: adjtimex(0x8001) failed : Operation not permitted
Aug 28 13:57:25 6350b2e720e9 systemd[1]: chronyd.service: Control process exited, code=exited status=1
Aug 28 13:57:25 6350b2e720e9 systemd[1]: chronyd.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Aug 28 13:57:25 6350b2e720e9 systemd[1]: Failed to start NTP client/server.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
I use roootles mode, and try to run container in rootless mode:
`podman run -d --cap-add=ALL --name rocky mymirror.localhost/rocky8`
`podman run -d --cap-add=CAP_SYS_TIME --name rocky mymirror.localhost/rocky8` 
`podman run -d --cap-add=ALL --privileged --name rocky mymirror.localhost/rocky8`
In all cases, service chronyd.service doesn`t work:

`[podman@worker ~]$ podman exec -it rocky bash`
`[root@6350b2e720e9 /]# systemctl status chronyd.service`
● chronyd.service - NTP client/server
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/system/chronyd.service; enabled; vendor preset: enabled)
   Active: failed (Result: exit-code) since Mon 2023-08-28 13:57:25 MSK; 13s ago
     Docs: man:chronyd(8)
           man:chrony.conf(5)
  Process: 29 ExecStopPost=/usr/libexec/chrony-helper remove-daemon-state (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
  Process: 20 ExecStart=/usr/sbin/chronyd $OPTIONS (code=exited, status=1/FAILURE)

Aug 28 13:57:25 6350b2e720e9 systemd[1]: Starting NTP client/server...
Aug 28 13:57:25 6350b2e720e9 chronyd[20]: adjtimex(0x8001) failed : Operation not permitted
Aug 28 13:57:25 6350b2e720e9 systemd[1]: chronyd.service: Control process exited, code=exited status=1
Aug 28 13:57:25 6350b2e720e9 systemd[1]: chronyd.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Aug 28 13:57:25 6350b2e720e9 systemd[1]: Failed to start NTP client/server.`


### Describe the results you received

In all cases, service chronyd.service doesn`t work:

Service can`t get access to adjtimex(0x8001):
Aug 28 13:57:25 6350b2e720e9 chronyd[20]: adjtimex(0x8001) failed : Operation not permitted

### Describe the results you expected

In rootless mode, service chronyd.service can start in container.

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @sema200.

>  Aug 28 13:57:25 6350b2e720e9 chronyd[20]: adjtimex(0x8001) failed : Operation not permitted

I am pretty sure this requires root privileges as it alters kernel data.  Can you try running the container as the root user?

Hello @vrothberg, thank you for you answer!
In root mode, service start and work:
`[root@worker ~] podman run -d --cap-add=ALL --name rocky mymirror.localhost/rocky8`
`[root@worker ~]# podman exec -it rocky bash`
`[root@cc8a85b156b6 /]# systemctl status chronyd.service`
● chronyd.service - NTP client/server
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/system/chronyd.service; enabled; vendor preset: enabled)
   Active: active (running) since Mon 2023-08-28 14:28:28 MSK; 34s ago
     Docs: man:chronyd(8)

Thanks for checking, @sema200.

In this case, there is nothing Podman can do as the workload requires true root that cannot be mapped by a user namespace.

I use jenkins + podman, and i test my roles in molecule.
When i user docker (docker\`s daemon start on root), i don\`t have this problems, after migration to Rocky8, and to podman,
i catch this problems.
@vrothberg thank you for you time!

@sema200, docker already runs as root. Podman runs rootless by default (for security reasons, among many others). It's rare that workloads require running as true root. In your case, add a `sudo podman` and it should work without issues.

@vrothberg Thank you for you help!
i will try use `sudo podman`.
